messages.content: You are TheOrc, a boss AI orchestrator. Your job is to decompose the user's goal into 2-4 concrete, non-overlapping worker tasks. Respond only with valid JSON matching this schema exactly: {"plan": "one sentence describing approach", "tasks": [{"role": "RESEARCHER|CODER|TESTER|DOCS", "priority": 1, "title": "Short title naming the output file", "description": "Detailed, self-contained instructions with API names, filenames, and acceptance criteria."}]}. Do not add prose outside the JSON. Do not wrap in markdown. Do not produce fewer than 2 tasks.
Goal: Build a Python CLI tool that watches a directory for new CSV files and automatically runs a data cleaning script on each one, logging results to cleaned.log.

{"plan":"Researcher surveys watchdog and pandas APIs; two coders build the watcher and the cleaner separately.","tasks":[{"role":"RESEARCHER","priority":1,"title":"Research watchdog and pandas APIs","description":"Investigate watchdog.observers.Observer and watchdog.events.PatternMatchingEventHandler for directory monitoring — find the correct on_created() signature and how to filter for *.csv patterns. Research pandas.read_csv() and DataFrame.dropna() for cleaning. Summarize: exact import paths, event handler class name and required methods, and a minimal pandas cleaning pipeline that drops null rows. Return as a structured summary; do not write any files."},{"role":"CODER","priority":2,"title":"Write cleaner.py — CSV cleaning logic","description":"Create cleaner.py. Implement clean_csv(input_path: str) -> dict that: (1) reads the CSV with pandas.read_csv(input_path), (2) records original row count, (3) drops all rows with any null via DataFrame.dropna(), (4) saves cleaned file to <stem>_cleaned.csv in the same directory, (5) returns {\"input\": input_path, \"original_rows\": int, \"cleaned_rows\": int, \"removed_rows\": int}. Raises FileNotFoundError if path does not exist. No argparse — this module is imported by watcher.py."},{"role":"CODER","priority":2,"title":"Write watcher.py — directory monitor and log writer","description":"Create watcher.py. Accept one CLI argument: --watch-dir (default: current directory). Import clean_csv from cleaner. Implement CsvHandler(PatternMatchingEventHandler) with patterns=['*.csv'] and on_created(event) that calls clean_csv(event.src_path) and appends a single log line to cleaned.log: '<ISO8601_TIMESTAMP> cleaned <input_path>: removed <N> rows'. Start a watchdog Observer on the watch directory. Print 'Watching <dir>. Press Ctrl+C to stop.' on startup. Handle KeyboardInterrupt cleanly by calling observer.stop() and observer.join(). Validation: run 'python watcher.py --watch-dir .' and drop a test CSV into the watched directory — confirm a _cleaned.csv appears and cleaned.log is updated."}]}

metadata.notes: Three-task plan. RESEARCHER task separates API discovery from coding. Clean API contract: cleaner.py is imported by watcher.py — no overlap. Validation command included in watcher task.
